compiled_state.title: As a developer, all five polyglot triggers are validated against a cross-stack test matrix and emit conformant TRIGGER_SKIPPED signals
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Validate all five polyglot triggers (T7, T9, T12, T13, T4) end-to-end against a cross-stack test matrix covering Ruby and JS/TS projects. Verify that TRIGGER_SKIPPED signals from all five triggers conform to the Kudos Epic 2 schema (no shared library import). Confirm that lizard and jscpd are installed in the sprint acceptance environment.

**Why**: Individual trigger stories (A-D) validate each trigger in isolation with mocks. This story proves the full system works end-to-end on real non-Python projects — exactly the gap Epic C aims to close.

**Scope**:
- IN: Acceptance test matrix: for each of the 5 triggers, a Ruby or JS/TS file fixture that meets the trigger condition (quality improvement) produces an award; a fixture that does not meet the condition produces no award; Kudos Epic 1's dispatch_kudos_trigger() is present or mocked in acceptance env; lizard and jscpd installed in CI/sprint acceptance env; TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal schema audit against Kudos Epic 2 spec
- OUT: Installing lizard/jscpd as system-wide packages (CI infrastructure); re-implementing trigger logic (covered by Stories A-D)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the test matrix passes: for each of T7, T9, T12, T13, T4 — a Ruby or JS/TS fixture that meets the trigger condition produces a kudos award; a fixture that does not meet the condition produces no award
  ← Satisfies: "Validation: test matrix covers all 5 triggers before/after on non-Python files; before: no kudos for Ruby file; after: kudos fires for quality improvement"
- When this story is complete, a TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal audit confirms all five triggers' TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal shapes match the Kudos Epic 2 (71df-5bd8) schema, and none import any module from Kudos Epic 2 source
  ← Satisfies: "All five triggers emit machine-readable TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal conforming to the signal format established by Kudos Epic 2; no shared library imported from Kudos Epic 2"
- When this story is complete, the sprint acceptance environment has lizard (pip-installable) and jscpd (npm-installable) available; confirmed by `lizard --version` and `jscpd --version` exiting 0 in CI
  ← Satisfies: "sprint acceptance test environment has lizard and jscpd installed"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for the signal conformance audit logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Kudos Epic 1 (3e9b-afee) must be present or dispatch_kudos_trigger() mocked at the acceptance-test level — document the test environment requirement explicitly
- [Testing] Use fixture files (not real project repos) for the test matrix — create minimal Ruby/JS/TS files that represent before/after states for each trigger condition
- [Integration] If dispatch_kudos_trigger() cannot be mocked at the integration test level, escalate — this may require coordination with Kudos Epic 1's implementation

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
